title: nasa rugosa subsp. llaqtacochaensis
intro: Nasa rugosa subsp. llaqtacochaensis, also known as Llaqtacochaensis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, endemic to Peru. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves, which are traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Nasa rugosa subsp. llaqtacochaensis include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared by infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea or by using them in tinctures and extracts for various health issues.
